interfaces.name: AppRoute
interfaces.signature: enum AppRoute: Equatable { case entry, setup, session, recap }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: root switch, no NavigationStack anywhere in the session flow — interactive back-swipe explicitly disabled.

interfaces.name: RoundPhase
interfaces.signature: enum RoundPhase: String, Codable { case handoff, ready, revealed }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: persisted on Session, not transient @State — required for true termination-and-relaunch resume.

interfaces.name: Player
interfaces.signature: struct Player: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var name: String; let createdAt: Date }
interfaces.notes: Display and stable identity for all session transitions; names can duplicate but cannot be blank/whitespace.

interfaces.name: PromptPack
interfaces.signature: struct PromptPack: Identifiable, Codable { let id: String; let title: String; let version: Int; let mode: String; let isBuiltIn: Bool; let prompts: [Prompt] }
interfaces.notes: Source content model loaded from bundled JSON and versioned for migration checks.

interfaces.name: Prompt
interfaces.signature: struct Prompt: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; let packId: String; let mode: String; let text: String }
interfaces.notes: Single immutable prompt item consumed by rounds in-order.

interfaces.name: SessionState
interfaces.signature: struct SessionState: Identifiable, Codable, Hashable { let id: UUID; var packId: String; var playerIDs: [UUID]; var roundOrder: [UUID]; var activePlayerID: UUID; var targetHistory: [UUID]; var tally: [UUID:Int]; var promptCursor: Int; var currentRound: Int; var totalRounds: Int; var phase: RoundPhase; var isCompleted: Bool; var startedAt: Date; var completedAt: Date? }
interfaces.notes: Source of truth for gameplay and resume safety. playerIDs and targetHistory must remain ordered arrays, never to-many relationships.

interfaces.name: GameRulesEngine
interfaces.signature: enum GameRulesEngine { static func nextActivePlayerIndex(players: [PlayerRecord], currentActiveIndex: Int) -> Int; static func nextTarget(players: [PlayerRecord], activeID: UUID, revealedRounds: [RevealedRoundRecord]) -> UUID; static func shufflePromptOrder(prompts: [PromptRecord]) -> [String]; static func deriveRecap(players: [PlayerRecord], revealedRounds: [RevealedRoundRecord]) -> RecapOutcome }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: static-function enum, not protocol — pure math, no state, no swappable implementations needed. shufflePromptOrder closes the prompt-variety gap (session's prompt sequence is randomized once at creation, not fixed bundled-JSON order). Fully unit-testable at 2/3/4/15 players with zero view hierarchy involved.

interfaces.name: RecapResult
interfaces.signature: enum RecapResult { case standout([UUID]), tie([UUID]), lowVariance(String), zeroData(String) }
interfaces.notes: Four-case recap semantics (clear standout, tie, low-variance/funny, zero-data) used for card generation and fallback.

interfaces.name: SessionRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol SessionRepository { func loadInProgress() throws -> SessionState?; func save(_ state: SessionState) throws; func delete(_ stateID: UUID) throws; func loadRecap(sessionID: UUID) throws -> RecapRow?; func saveRecap(_ recap: RecapRow) throws }
interfaces.notes: Persistence seam backed by SwiftData; all mutations are synchronous and write immediately.

interfaces.name: PackRepository
interfaces.signature: protocol PackRepository { func loadPacks() throws -> [PromptPack]; func loadPack(id: String) throws -> PromptPack?; func ensureSeedDataVersion() throws -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: Loads only bundled JSON packs for v1; future remote catalog can swap implementation without changing callers.

interfaces.name: ContentAccessProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ContentAccessProviding { func isPackUnlocked(_ packID: String) -> Bool; func canSaveUnlimitedRecaps() -> Bool }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: stub always returns true, explicitly flagged provisional, not real entitlement policy.

interfaces.name: RecapRenderer
interfaces.signature: protocol RecapRenderer { func renderImage(for recap: RecapSnapshot, palette: PaletteTokenSet, size: CGSize) throws -> Data }
interfaces.notes: Isolated service for ImageRenderer output at fixed frame and scale with deterministic snapshot pipeline.

interfaces.name: HapticsService
interfaces.signature: protocol HapticsService { func triggerReveal(); func triggerReadyHint(); func triggerError(); func setEnabled(_ isEnabled: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: UI-only side effects service; guarded to one-shot per transition.

interfaces.name: SessionViewModel
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class SessionViewModel { private(set) var session: Session; private(set) var isTransitioning: Bool; func beginHandoff(); func advancePastHandoff(); func revealTarget(); func advanceRound(); func endSessionEarly() }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: wraps the persisted Session @Model directly and calls context.save() itself — no SessionStoreProviding seam (explicitly cut both agents agreed). Unit tests use an in-memory ModelConfiguration, not a mock protocol, so the real save path is what's actually exercised.

interfaces.name: CampfireError
interfaces.signature: enum CampfireError: Error, Equatable { case storeUnavailable; case emptyPromptPack; case recapRenderFailure }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: exactly the three recoverable states locked across prior phases. No associated payload strings — the UI layer owns copy per case.

interfaces.name: PlayerRecord
interfaces.signature: struct PlayerRecord: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: UUID; var name: String }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: single ordered array element for player identity/order — one array, no parallel name lookup that could drift.

interfaces.name: TargetHistoryEntry
interfaces.signature: struct TargetHistoryEntry: Codable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let targetPlayerID: UUID }
interfaces.notes: Lightweight history event for recap derivation, and supports low-variance copy fallback.

interfaces.name: RevealedRoundRecord
interfaces.signature: struct RevealedRoundRecord: Codable, Equatable { let roundIndex: Int; let activePlayerID: UUID; let targetPlayerID: UUID; let promptID: String; let promptText: String }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: sole history array — no separate targetHistory/TargetHistoryEntry, no stored tally. nextTarget derives recency from revealedRounds.map(\.targetPlayerID); deriveRecap derives tally, tie, and the specific low-variance moment from this same array.

interfaces.name: RecapOutcome
interfaces.signature: enum RecapOutcome: Codable, Equatable { case standout(playerID: UUID, line: String); case tie(playerIDs: [UUID], line: String); case lowVariance(momentLine: String); case zeroData(line: String) }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: embedded directly on Session, not a separate RecapEntry model. Doubles as card headline content and the always-available fallback text on render failure. No persisted image blob — card is re-rendered on demand, including after resume-from-kill.

interfaces.name: Session
interfaces.signature: @Model final class Session { var id: UUID; var players: [PlayerRecord]; var currentRoundIndex: Int; var activePlayerIndex: Int; var roundPhase: RoundPhase; var promptOrder: [String]; var revealedRounds: [RevealedRoundRecord]; var packID: String; var isComplete: Bool; var recapOutcome: RecapOutcome? }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: direct SwiftData model, no repository/state-struct split. activePlayerIndex is an Int into the fixed players array. promptOrder is the shuffled prompt-ID sequence locked at session creation; revealedRounds.count is the cursor into it (no separate stored cursor field).

interfaces.name: PromptRecord
interfaces.signature: struct PromptRecord: Codable, Identifiable, Equatable { let id: String; var text: String }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: decoded from bundled JSON, stored as a value-type array on PromptPackRecord.

interfaces.name: PromptPackRecord
interfaces.signature: @Model final class PromptPackRecord { var id: String; var displayName: String; var version: Int; var prompts: [PromptRecord] }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: version:Int folded directly onto the pack, no separate PackVersion row for one bundled pack.

interfaces.name: PlayerSetupValidation
interfaces.signature: enum PlayerSetupValidation { static func validate(rawNames: [String]) -> PlayerSetupValidationResult }; enum PlayerSetupValidationResult: Equatable { case valid([String]); case invalid(rowErrors: [Int: String], continueDisabledReason: String?) }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: 2-15 players, blank/whitespace rejected per-row, no auto-fix.

interfaces.name: RecoverableError
interfaces.signature: enum RecoverableError: Equatable { case emptyPromptPack; case recapRenderFailure; case storeUnavailable }
interfaces.notes: CONVERGED: matches Codex's CampfireError cases (modelContainerUnavailable/emptyPack/renderFailure map 1:1); naming needs a final pick round 2 but the three cases are agreed.

interfaces.name: AppCoordinator
interfaces.signature: @MainActor @Observable final class AppCoordinator { var route: AppRoute; var activeError: CampfireError?; func startSetup(); func beginSession(with players: [PlayerRecord]); func finishToRecap(); func exitToEntry(); func resumeInProgressSessionIfAny(context: ModelContext) }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: concrete class, not a protocol — exactly one real implementation exists. Owns navigation/route only; resumeInProgressSessionIfAny is its own separately tested launch path, not a fallthrough of the fresh-start path.

interfaces.name: PromptRepositoryProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol PromptRepositoryProviding { func loadDefaultPackIfNeeded(context: ModelContext) throws; func activePrompts(for packID: String, context: ModelContext) throws -> [PromptRecord] }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: throws surface as CampfireError.emptyPromptPack at the call site if decode/load fails or yields zero prompts. This protocol earns its keep — a future remote-catalog implementation is a named future requirement that would genuinely conform to it.

interfaces.name: ModelContainerProvider
interfaces.signature: enum ModelContainerProvider { static func makeContainer() -> ModelContainerResult }; enum ModelContainerResult { case success(ModelContainer); case failure(Error) }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: failure path feeds CampfireError.storeUnavailable and an in-memory fallback container, never a crash.

interfaces.name: HapticsServicing
interfaces.signature: protocol HapticsServicing { func handoffBuzz(); func revealBeat(); func setEnabled(_ isEnabled: Bool) }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: only the two locked haptic moments (handoff auto-advance, reveal beat) plus the optional on/off toggle from app_features' 'could' list.

interfaces.name: RecapCardRendering
interfaces.signature: protocol RecapCardRendering { @MainActor func renderImage(for session: Session) async -> RecapRenderResult }; enum RecapRenderResult { case success(imageData: Data); case failure }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: fixed 9:16 portrait render frame, capped '+N more' player footer, rendered via ImageRenderer at 3x scale after a confirmed layout pass. Not persisted as a blob — re-rendered on demand from Session, including after resume-from-kill. Must be verified on a physical device.

interfaces.name: TransitionGuard
interfaces.signature: final class TransitionGuard { private(set) var isLocked: Bool; func tryLock() -> Bool; func unlock() }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: silent double-tap guard, no user-visible 'blocked' messaging — the control just disables itself.

interfaces.name: ConfettiOverlay
interfaces.signature: struct ConfettiOverlay: View { let isActive: Bool }
interfaces.notes: LOCKED: hard-capped 500-700ms lifecycle, torn down immediately after burst, never redraws into later rounds.

interfaces.name: RecapEntry
interfaces.signature: struct RecapEntry: Identifiable { let id: UUID; let sessionID: UUID; let packId: String; let promptCount: Int; let winnerText: String; let standings: [RecapPlayerStat]; let createdAt: Date; let cardImageData: Data?; let fallbackText: String }
interfaces.notes: Codex's separate persisted recap row — OPEN CONFLICT with Claude's embedded Session.recapOutcome. Round 2 must pick exactly one recap-persistence shape.

interfaces.name: PlayerSetupValidationResult
interfaces.signature: enum PlayerSetupValidationResult: Equatable { case valid([String]); case invalid(rowErrors: [Int: String], continueDisabledReason: String?) }
interfaces.notes: No auto-fix suggestions; UI blocks Continue with a specific reason.

interfaces.name: SessionStoreProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol SessionStoreProviding { func loadInProgressSession() throws -> Session?; func saveSession(_ session: Session) throws; func deleteSession(_ id: UUID) throws; func loadPromptPack(id: String) throws -> PromptPackRecord?; func ensureSeedPackLoaded() throws }
interfaces.notes: OPEN: Codex wants this as a thin persistence seam; Claude argues it's unearned complexity since the real termination test must go through actual SwiftData anyway. Round 3 decides keep-or-cut.

interfaces.name: ConfettiLifecycle
interfaces.signature: enum ConfettiLifecycle { case stopped; case active(start: Date, durationMs: Int = 650) }
interfaces.notes: Hard cap 500–700ms, self-teardown at phase transition to avoid redraw leaks.

interfaces.name: ModelContainerProviding
interfaces.signature: protocol ModelContainerProviding { func makeContainer() -> ModelContainerResult }; enum ModelContainerResult { case success(ModelContainer); case failure(Error) }
interfaces.notes: Build-time/fallback startup branch: model-container failure is non-fatal and visible.

interfaces.name: ModelContainerResult
interfaces.signature: enum ModelContainerResult { case success(ModelContainer); case failure(Error) }
interfaces.notes: Container bootstrap contract. failure path must show user-visible recovery and use in-memory fallback.

interfaces.name: RecapRenderResult
interfaces.signature: enum RecapRenderResult { case success(imageData: Data); case failure }
interfaces.notes: Render result from ImageRenderer service. On failure, fallback text path is always available via Session.recapOutcome.
